title: Viral enrichment from wastewater samples
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bg0a9","text":"Wastewater samples may contain potencially harmful pathogens, so always handle with gloves, work in a laminar flow cabinet, and pasteurize samples immediately after filtration. All equipment used up to this step must be washed in hypochlorite. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wastewater samples are centrifuged at 4500 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes to remove debris Volume processed depends on user preferences and equipment. For information on sampling, check our previous protocol : Sampling and viral concentration for SARS-CoV-2 detection in wastewater

The supernatant is then filtered through a 0.45 µm membrane to remove eukaryotic and bacterial cells Although the first step is optional, if your sample is very cloudy, it will take a lot of time for it to flow through the filter. Also, if you see the flow stops, your membrane might be clogged, and you should replace it.

25 mL of the flowthrough are then transferred to a 26,9 mL ultracentrifuge tube (compatible with Beckman Coulter Type 50.2 Ti Fixed-Angle Rotor) and spiked with 5.9 µL of PP7 phage (this enacts as a protocol validation method later in the analysis). Make sure your tubes are correctly balanced before you set up the Ultracentrifuge, according to manufacturer's instructions
The pellet is then resuspended in 200 µL of Zymo DNA/RNA Shield, and stored at -80 °C or used for DNA/RNA extraction DNA/RNA Shield Zymo Research Catalog #R1100-50
Then, the result is ultracentrifuged at 100000 x g, 4 °C, 2 hours 30 minutes Ultracentrifuge (Beckman Coulter, XPN-90, Your ultracentrifuge must reach 100,000 x g and cool down to 4º C)

The flowthrough is pasteurized to inactivate pathogens at 60 °C for 01:30:00 Wastewater samples may contain potencially harmful pathogens, so always handle with gloves, work in a laminar flow cabinet, and pasteurize samples immediately after filtration. All equipment used up to this step must be washed in hypochlorite.
creator: Iraola Lab
